title: Bettok
content:

*[[New Territories/Legends|New Territories]]<ref name="New Territories">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} places the [[Bettok system/Legends|Bettok system]], and therefore Bettok itself, in the [[Velcar sector/Legends|Velcar sector]]. {{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-outer-rim-sectors|text=The Essential Atlas Extra: Outer Rim Sectors|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} further places that [[Sector/Legends|sector]] in the region ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' identifies as the [[New Territories/Legends|New Territories]].</ref>

'''Bettok''' was an astronomical object<ref name="DFRS" /> located in the [[Bettok system/Legends|Bettok system]], a part of the [[Velcar sector/Legends|Velcar sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in the [[New Territories/Legends|New Territories]] portion of<ref name="New Territories" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" /> The [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[TIE pilot|TIE fighter pilot]] [[Creb]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] at Bettok<ref name="DFRS" /> in [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]].<ref name="Date">''[[Dark Force Rising Sourcebook]]'', which is set during the events of ''[[Dark Force Rising]]'', states that [[Creb]] is 29 [[Standard year/Legends|years]] old. ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the latter work to [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], so Creb must have been [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] in [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]].</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Bettok was mentioned in ''[[Dark Force Rising Sourcebook]]'', a [[1992]] supplement authored by [[Bill Slavicsek]] for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="DFRS" /> The [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|Online Companion]] to the [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Bettok [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the celestial body itself, in grid square J-4.<ref name="EAOC" />
